auth.forgotPasswordDesc: Enter your email and we'll send you a reset link.
auth.twoFactorDesc: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.
auth.forgotPasswordSuccess: If an account exists with that email address, we've sent a password reset link. Please check your inbox.
auth.passwordMinError: Password must be at least 8 characters.
auth.passwordResetRedirecting: Password reset successfully! Redirecting to login...
auth.invalidResetToken: Invalid or missing reset token. Please request a new password reset link.
auth.enterBackupCode: Enter one of your backup codes

auth.emailVerifiedDesc: Your email has been successfully verified. You can now sign in.

domains.customNsWarning: Using custom nameservers means DNS records managed in Fleet won't resolve for this domain.

domains.verificationFailedDetail: Verification failed. Please check your DNS records and try again.

volumeSharing.allServicesShare: All services will share a single storage volume

storagePage.name: Name
storagePage.nameHint: Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only.
storagePage.confirmDelete: Delete this volume? All data will be lost.
storagePage.shrinkWarning: Shrinking a volume cannot be undone. Make sure you have backups.

storageSetup.title: Storage
storageSetup.replicationDesc: Your data is replicated across {factor} nodes. Up to {failCount} node(s) can fail without data loss.
storageSetup.noStorageDesc: Set up your storage system to enable volume replication, object storage for uploads and backups, and automatic failover across nodes.
storageSetup.chooseModeDesc: Select how you want Fleet to manage storage across your infrastructure.
storageSetup.localDesc: For development or single-server setups. No replication. Uses local filesystem and NFS. This is the current default.
storageSetup.distributedDesc: Production-grade. Replicates data across 3+ nodes with automatic failover and self-healing.
storageSetup.glusterfsDesc: Distributed replicated filesystem. Great for bare metal and VMs. POSIX-compatible, auto-heal, Docker volume plugin.
storageSetup.cephDesc: Enterprise-grade block storage. Scales to exabytes. Erasure coding, snapshots, thin provisioning. Industry standard.
storageSetup.minioDesc: Self-hosted S3-compatible. Runs on your own nodes. No vendor lock-in.
storageSetup.s3Desc: Amazon S3 or S3-compatible services (Backblaze B2, Wasabi, etc.)
storageSetup.gcsDesc: Google Cloud Storage. Native GCS integration for GCP deployments.
storageSetup.addNodesDesc: Add at least {count} nodes to form the storage cluster. Each node stores a full copy of your data.
storageSetup.localNoNodes: Local mode uses the current server. No additional nodes needed.
storageSetup.localModeInfo: Local mode uses this server's filesystem. All volumes and objects are stored on a single node. You can upgrade to distributed mode later.
storageSetup.nodeSetupGuideDesc: Prerequisites, firewall ports, security, and step-by-step instructions
storageSetup.overviewPart1: Each storage node is a server (bare metal or VM) that stores and replicates your data. Nodes can be
storageSetup.overviewMinNodes: You need at least {count} nodes for {count}-way replication.
storageSetup.staticIpDesc: Each storage node must have a fixed IP address (static assignment or DHCP reservation). Changing IPs will break the storage cluster.
storageSetup.privateNetworkDesc: Storage traffic should stay on a private/internal network (e.g. 10.0.x.x). Do not expose storage ports to the public internet.
storageSetup.lowLatencyDesc: All storage nodes should be in the same datacenter or availability zone. High latency will degrade performance and replication speed.
storageSetup.storageOnlyNoSwarm: Storage-only nodes do NOT need Docker Swarm.
storageSetup.storageOnlyNoSwarmDesc: Dedicated storage nodes run only the storage services ({services}). They are managed by Fleet over SSH.
storageSetup.storageComputeSwarm: Storage+Compute nodes are part of Docker Swarm.
storageSetup.storageComputeSwarmDesc: These nodes join the swarm as workers AND run storage. Use this when you want fewer servers doing both jobs.
storageSetup.swarmNeedsClient: Swarm worker nodes need the volume client.
storageSetup.swarmNeedsClientDesc: Any Docker Swarm node that runs containers with storage volumes needs the {client} installed so it can mount volumes.
storageSetup.firewallPortsDesc: Open these ports between storage nodes and from Swarm workers to storage nodes. Only allow traffic from your internal network.
storageSetup.sshServiceDesc: SSH — Fleet manages nodes via SSH
storageSetup.neverExposePorts: Never expose storage ports ({ports}) to the public internet. Use ufw or iptables to restrict access to your internal network CIDR only.
storageSetup.secSshKeyPart2: (disable password login). Fleet connects to nodes via SSH for management operations.
storageSetup.secVlanPart2: separate from public-facing services if possible.
storageSetup.secGlusterfsPart2: . Ensure only trusted IPs can reach port 24007. Consider TLS encryption for GlusterFS (available in v3.12+).
storageSetup.secCephPart2: by default. Keep the keyring file secure and restrict access to the admin user.
storageSetup.secMinioPart2: for production. Use strong access key and secret key (auto-generated during setup).
storageSetup.secUpdatesPart1: Keep the operating system and storage software
storageSetup.secUpdatesPart2: with security patches. Enable unattended-upgrades for automatic security updates.
storageSetup.quickSetup: Quick Setup — Run on Each Storage Node
storageSetup.quickSetupDesc: SSH into each node and run the following commands to prepare it for Fleet storage.
storageSetup.swarmWorkersInstall: On Docker Swarm workers (so containers can mount volumes):
storageSetup.configureReplicationDesc: Set the replication factor and object storage settings.
storageSetup.threeWayDesc: Survives 2 node failures. Recommended for production.
storageSetup.customEndpointHint: for S3-compatible services like Backblaze B2, Wasabi
storageSetup.gcsWorkloadIdentityNote: If running on GCP with workload identity, you can leave the key file empty. The GCS client will use the default credentials automatically.
storageSetup.localNoConfig: Local mode requires no additional configuration. Storage will use the local filesystem on this server.
storageSetup.initializeDesc: This will set up the storage system and verify everything is working.
storageSetup.clickInitDistributed: Click the button below to initialize the GlusterFS cluster and MinIO.
storageSetup.clickInitLocal: Click the button below to initialize the local storage system.
storageSetup.migrateExistingDesc: Move your existing volumes, uploads, and backups to the new storage system.
storageSetup.noDataToMigrate: No existing data to migrate. You can skip this step.
storageSetup.migrationOnlineNotice: Services will continue running during migration. A brief switchover happens at the end when the active provider is changed.
storageSetup.readyDistributed: Your storage system is ready. All volumes and objects will now use the distributed cluster.
storageSetup.readyLocal: Your storage system is ready. All volumes and objects will now use the local filesystem.
storageSetup.migrateStorageProviderDesc: Move all data between storage providers with zero downtime.
storageSetup.servicesOnline: Services will remain online during migration
storageSetup.briefSwitchover: A brief switchover occurs when data is fully copied
storageSetup.pauseResume: You can pause and resume the migration at any time
storageSetup.rollbackAvailable: Rollback is available if anything goes wrong
storageSetup.migrationCompleteDesc: All data has been migrated to the new providers. The storage system is now using {volumeProvider} for volumes and {objectProvider} for objects.
storageSetup.readyAutoConfigPending: Ready — {auto} optional port(s) will be configured during setup
storageSetup.requiredNotRunning: Required — install this service before proceeding
storageSetup.migrationFailedCheckLog: Migration failed. Check the log for details.
storageSetup.attachNodeDesc: Add a new storage node to the existing cluster.
storageSetup.detachNodeDesc: Remove an individual node from the cluster. If using GlusterFS, the peer will be detached first.
storageSetup.detachConfirmDesc: This will remove the node from the storage cluster. Existing data on remaining nodes will not be affected, but replication factor may be reduced.
storageSetup.resetAllDesc: Remove all storage nodes and reset the cluster configuration. This does not delete stored data on the nodes themselves.
storageSetup.resetConfirmTitle: Are you sure you want to reset all storage?
storageSetup.resetConfirmDesc: This will remove all storage nodes from the database and delete the cluster configuration. You will need to reconfigure storage from scratch. Enter your password to confirm.

backups.noBackupsDesc: Create a backup to protect your data.
backups.confirmRestore: Restore from this backup? This will overwrite current data.

marketplace.title: App Marketplace
marketplace.noEnvVars: No environment variables. Click + Add to configure.
marketplace.resourceLimitDisclaimer: Resource limits per container: max {cpu} millicores CPU, {memory} MB memory, {replicas} replicas. Values exceeding account limits will be capped.
marketplace.estimatedCost: Estimated monthly cost: {cost}/mo (based on current pricing rates)
marketplace.composeEditorHint: Advanced: Edit the raw YAML template. Changes here override the template defaults.
marketplace.deployPartial: {name} deployed, but {count} service(s) failed to start. Check the services page for details.
marketplace.noPricing: Pricing not configured. Contact your administrator to set up billing rates.

deploy.title: Deploy
deploy.dockerImageDesc: Deploy from a Docker Hub or private registry image.
deploy.fromGithubDesc: Deploy from a GitHub repository with automatic builds.
deploy.fromMarketplaceDesc: Deploy a pre-configured app from the marketplace.
deploy.noPorts: No ports exposed. Add a port mapping to make the service accessible.
deploy.noVolumes: No volumes. Add one to persist data across restarts.
deploy.dbWithoutVolumeDesc: Without a volume, data will be lost when the container restarts. Consider adding a volume mounted to {path}.

github.connectDesc: Connect your GitHub account to browse your repositories and deploy directly from your codebase with automatic builds.
github.autoDeployDesc: Automatically redeploy when new commits are pushed to the selected branch.

deploy.fromUploadDesc: Upload a project archive (zip, tar) or folder and deploy it.
deploy.dragDrop: Drop your archive here or click to browse
deploy.buildFileHint: Dockerfile or docker-compose.yml path. Leave empty to auto-detect, or skip if not needed yet.

deployGh.title: Deploy from GitHub
deployGh.noManifest: This repository does not include a fleet.json manifest. You can still deploy it, but you will need to configure environment variables and ports manually.

deployWizard.title: Deploy {name}
deployWizard.subtitle: Step-by-step deployment wizard
deployWizard.multiServiceInfo: All {count} services will be deployed on a shared network and can communicate with each other automatically. Credentials and connection details are pre-configured.
deployWizard.noRequiredConfig: This template has no required configuration. All secrets are auto-generated.

config.domainConfigurationDesc: Optionally assign domains to your services. You can also configure this later.
config.securityCredentialsDesc: Passwords and secrets are auto-generated. You can customize them below.
config.passwordsSafetyNotice: All passwords are securely generated. Save them somewhere safe — you'll need them to connect to your services.
config.noConfiguration: This template has no configuration — it's ready to deploy with defaults!

storage.label: Storage
storage.title: Storage Configuration
storage.description: Configure volumes for persistent data. Create new volumes or attach existing ones.
storage.quotaExceededDesc: Reduce volume sizes, use existing volumes, or upgrade your plan to continue.

resources.description: Configure replicas, CPU, and memory for each service. Leave CPU/Memory at 0 to use platform defaults.

fileExplorer.rebuildDesc: Upload a new archive to replace source files and trigger a rebuild.

service.githubBranchHint: The branch to watch for pushes and PR merges.
service.autoDeployDesc: Automatically deploy when code is pushed or a PR is merged into this branch.

robots.title: Robots.txt
robots.desc: Control how web crawlers access this service. Only shown for services with a domain.
robots.useDefaultDesc: Serve the platform-wide robots.txt configured by the admin.
robots.customDesc: Provide a custom robots.txt for this service.
robots.disabledDesc: Don't intercept /robots.txt — let the service container respond directly.

settings.twoFactorDesc: Add an extra layer of security to your account.
settings.scanQrCode: Scan the QR code with your authenticator app
settings.enterCode: Enter the code from your app to confirm
settings.backupCodesDesc: Save these codes in a safe place. Each code can only be used once.
settings.deleteAccountDesc: Schedule your account for deletion. All services will be stopped immediately and the account will be permanently deleted after 30 days.
settings.confirmDelete: This action cannot be undone. All your services, domains, and data will be permanently deleted.
settings.deleteConfirmDesc: Your account will be scheduled for deletion in 30 days. All running services will be stopped immediately. You can cancel the deletion at any time during the grace period.
settings.passwordRequired: Password is required to confirm deletion
settings.deletionScheduled: Account has been scheduled for deletion
settings.deletionScheduledDesc: This account will be permanently deleted on {date} ({days} days remaining). All services have been stopped.
settings.deletionScheduledNote: You can cancel the deletion at any time before the scheduled date. After deletion, all data will be permanently removed.
settings.disconnectConfirm: Are you sure you want to disconnect this account from its parent? This cannot be undone.
settings.slugHint: This is used in URLs and API calls.
settings.disconnectParentDesc: Remove this account from its parent organization. This cannot be undone.
settings.registryCredentialsDesc: Connect container registries to pull private images during deployment.
settings.removeCredentialConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove this registry credential?

profile.name: Name
profile.avatarHint: JPEG, PNG, GIF or WebP. Max 2 MB.

profile.avatarInvalidType: Invalid file type. Use JPEG, PNG, GIF or WebP
profile.verificationSent: Verification email sent to {email}. Check your inbox and click the link to confirm.
profile.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 8 characters
profile.connectedAccountsDesc: Link third-party accounts for quick login.
profile.loginMethodsDesc: Control which methods can be used to sign into your account.
profile.cantDisableLast: Cannot disable — this is your only login method
profile.dataExportDesc: Download a copy of all your data (GDPR).
profile.dataExportDetails: This export includes your profile, account memberships, services, deployments, SSH keys, API keys, domains, and activity log.
profile.deleteAccountDesc: Permanently delete your account and all associated data. This action cannot be undone.
profile.deleteAccountSoleOwner: If you are the sole member of any accounts, those accounts and their services will be suspended and scheduled for deletion after 30 days.
profile.deleteAccountVerify: This will permanently delete your account. Verify your identity to continue.
profile.verificationCodeSent: Verification code sent to your email
profile.enterPasswordOrCode: Enter your password or email verification code

nodes.nodeRegistered: Node registered. Run this on the new node to join the swarm:

services.noServices: No services found across any accounts.

users.name: Name
users.noPermissions: No permissions configured for this role.
users.superAdminAccess: Full access — super admins bypass all permission checks.

marketplace.noTemplatesDesc: Create templates that users can deploy with one click.
marketplace.name: Name
marketplace.deleteConfirm: Delete template "{slug}"? This cannot be undone.

settings.title: Platform Settings
settings.rootDomainDesc: The base domain for your platform (e.g. fleet.example.com). Used for SFTP, database connections, webhooks, and DNS.
settings.maxServicesPerAccountDesc: Maximum number of services each account can deploy.
settings.maxReplicasPerAccountDesc: Maximum total number of replicas across all services per account.
settings.domainRegistrarDesc: Configure domain registrar API for selling domains.
settings.domainPricingDesc: Configure prices for each TLD sold to customers.
settings.noTldPricing: No TLD pricing configured. Click "Sync from Provider" to fetch prices.
settings.githubConfigDesc: Configure GitHub OAuth for auto-deploy from repositories.
settings.githubOAuthHint: Create a GitHub OAuth App at github.com/settings/developers. Set the callback URL to your-domain.com/api/v1/auth/github/callback.
settings.googleConfigDesc: Configure Google OAuth to allow users to sign in with their Google account.
settings.googleOAuthHint: Create an OAuth 2.0 Client ID at console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials. Set the authorized redirect URI to your-domain.com/api/v1/auth/google/callback.
settings.brandingDesc: Customize your platform's logo, favicon, and name.
settings.logoHint: Recommended: PNG or SVG, max 2 MB
settings.faviconHint: Recommended: ICO or PNG 32x32, max 500 KB
settings.logArchivingDesc: Automatically archive old audit and error log entries to compressed files.
settings.logArchiveAllowUserDelete: Allow account owners to delete their own archives
settings.translationConfigDesc: Configure the translation service used for auto-translating status posts.

selfHealing.title: Self-Repair
selfHealing.titleDesc: Configure AI-powered self-repair for automated code fixes.
selfHealing.anthropicApiKeyDesc: API key for Claude Code CLI. Get one from
selfHealing.githubPatDesc: PAT with repo, workflow, and release permissions.
selfHealing.autoUpdateDesc: Update Fleet when release is ready
selfHealing.notConfigured: Self-repair is not configured. Add API keys in Settings.
selfHealing.confirmSelfHealDesc: This will run the full self-repair loop: fix the issue, create a PR, verify CI, merge, create a release, and upgrade the platform. Are you sure?

registry.title: Built-in Registry
registry.desc: The platform's built-in Docker registry, proxied through Traefik with TLS. Used for custom upload deployments.
registry.repairDesc: Force-restarts the registry service and verifies it becomes healthy.

orchestrator.title: Orchestrator
orchestrator.titleDesc: Choose the container orchestration backend for new and existing services. Both Docker Swarm and Kubernetes can run simultaneously.
orchestrator.defaultDesc: New services will be deployed on the default backend. Existing services are not affected.
orchestrator.migrateServiceDesc: Move an individual service from one orchestrator to another. The service will be recreated on the target backend with zero downtime.
orchestrator.installK3sDesc: Install k3s (lightweight Kubernetes) alongside Docker Swarm. Both can run simultaneously.
orchestrator.installK3sServerDesc: Installs the k3s control plane on the current node (1-2 minutes).
orchestrator.installK3sAgentsDesc: Installs the k3s agent on all worker nodes so they can run Kubernetes workloads.
orchestrator.installDockerDesc: Install Docker and initialize Swarm mode alongside Kubernetes. Both can run simultaneously.
orchestrator.installDockerServerDesc: Installs Docker and initializes Swarm as manager on the current node.
orchestrator.installDockerAgentsDesc: Installs Docker on all worker nodes and joins them to the Swarm cluster.

database.title: Database
database.desc: Manage where your platform database is stored. Pin it to a specific manager node or migrate to shared storage.
database.mismatchDetail: The database is running on {current} but should be pinned to {pinned}. This can cause data loss if the service restarts. Re-pin to the correct node immediately.
database.repinDesc: Re-pin the database and other stateful services (Valkey, Registry) to a different manager node. The services will be rescheduled to the target node.
database.migrateToNfsDesc: Move the database to an NFS storage cluster so it can run on any manager node without data loss.
database.migrateWarningDesc: The database will be temporarily unavailable during migration. All API requests will fail until the migration completes. Make sure no critical operations are in progress.
database.migrateConfirm: I understand this will cause downtime and want to proceed
database.migrateToLocalDesc: Move the database back to a local volume on a specific manager node. The database will be pinned to the selected node.
database.loadError: Could not load database status. Check that the API is reachable.

robots.title: Robots.txt
robots.desc: Configure the default robots.txt served for all services. Individual services can override or disable this.
robots.hint: This content is served at /robots.txt for the platform domain and all services using the default mode.

env.title: Environment
env.desc: View and manage server environment variables. These are stored in the config file and read by all platform services.
env.confirmDangerous: I understand changing this critical variable may break the platform

billing.title: Billing Configuration
billing.billingModelDesc: Choose how your platform charges customers.
billing.fixedPriceDesc: Flat rate pricing per plan tier
billing.allowUserChoice: Allow end users to choose their billing model
billing.planTiersDesc: Define pricing plans your customers can subscribe to.
billing.noPlans: No plans created yet. Click "Add Plan" to create your first tier.
billing.usagePricingDesc: Set per-unit rates for resource consumption.
billing.locationPricingDesc: Enable location pricing to configure per-region cost multipliers.
billing.label: Label
billing.resourceLimitsDesc: Default limits inherited by all accounts. Override per account as needed.
billing.overridesDesc: Custom discounts and pricing for specific accounts.
billing.stripePaymentMethods: Apple Pay, Google Pay & Fast Checkout
billing.stripePaymentMethodsDesc: Enable express payment methods so your customers can pay faster with Apple Pay, Google Pay, and Stripe Link.
billing.stripeStep1: Go to Settings > Payment methods in your Stripe Dashboard
billing.stripeStep2: Enable Apple Pay, Google Pay, and optionally Link (Stripe's fast checkout)
billing.stripeStep3: For Apple Pay, verify your domain under Settings > Payment methods > Apple Pay by adding the Stripe domain verification file
billing.dataLifecycleDesc: Configure automatic suspension, deletion, and permanent purge of account data.
billing.autoSuspend: Automatically suspend accounts with overdue payments
billing.autoDelete: Automatically schedule deletion of suspended accounts
billing.volumeDeletion: Delete storage volumes when account is deleted
billing.purgeEnabled: Permanently remove soft-deleted data after retention period

statusPosts.title: Status Posts
statusPosts.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this post? This cannot be undone.
statusPosts.bodyPlaceholder: Write your status update in markdown...
statusPosts.selectPost: Select a post from the list to edit it.
statusPosts.noApiKey: Translation service not configured. Add an API key in Settings > Translation.

sharedDomains.title: Shared Domains
sharedDomains.priceHint: Price in smallest currency unit (e.g. cents)
sharedDomains.empty: No shared domains configured. Add a domain to let users claim subdomains.
sharedDomains.confirmDelete: Delete this shared domain? All subdomain claims will be removed.
sharedDomains.dnsHint: For each shared domain, configure a wildcard DNS record (*.example.com) pointing to your platform IP. Traefik will automatically handle SSL certificates for each claimed subdomain.

translations.title: Translations
translations.description: Manage UI translations for all locales
translations.deleteLocaleConfirm: Delete the {name} ({code}) locale? All translations will be lost.
translations.translateNotConfigured: Translation API key not configured. Set it in Settings > Translation.
translations.selectLocale: Select a locale to view and edit translations

platformDb.title: Database
platformDb.subtitle: Browse and manage the platform database directly
platformDb.selectTable: Select a table to view its data.
platformDb.queryHint: Press Ctrl+Enter to execute. Full read/write access — be careful with destructive queries.
platformDb.sshTunnelDesc: If the database is not directly accessible, use an SSH tunnel to forward the port to your local machine:
platformDb.sshTunnelAfter: Then connect to localhost with the forwarded port using your preferred client.
platformDb.pgAdminDesc: Use the connection details above to add a new server in pgAdmin.
platformDb.dbeaverDesc: Create a new connection using the host, port, and credentials above.
platformDb.tablePlusDesc: Create a new connection and paste the connection string.
platformDb.allowlistInfo: Restrict remote database connections to specific IP addresses. This does not affect the web dashboard — only direct connections via CLI or external tools. Leave empty to allow all IPs.
platformDb.noAllowlistEntries: No IP restrictions — all IPs can connect remotely to the database.

setup.title: Fleet Setup
setup.subtitle: Set up your hosting platform in a few steps
setup.howToSetup: How would you like to set up this server?
setup.leaderDesc: Initialize a new Fleet platform. Create the first admin account, configure your domain, and start managing services.
setup.workerDesc: Join this server to an existing Fleet cluster. Install Docker, join the Swarm, and configure shared storage.
setup.dockerSwarmDesc: Fleet uses Docker Swarm to orchestrate your services.
setup.installDockerHint: Docker is not installed on this server.
setup.installDockerAutoHint: Run the Fleet install script to automatically install Docker and all dependencies:
setup.runJoinScript: Run the Fleet join script on this server. It will automatically install Docker, NFS, and all required dependencies:
setup.joinScriptNote: The join script handles everything: system dependencies, Docker installation, NFS client, and Swarm join configuration.
setup.manualDockerNote: Or if you prefer to install Docker manually:
setup.infraDesc: Select how Fleet will manage your containers. You can use one or both.
setup.orchDockerDesc: Traditional container orchestration with built-in clustering and overlay networking.
setup.orchK8sDesc: Lightweight k3s distribution. Industry-standard container orchestration.
setup.orchBothDesc: Run Docker Swarm and Kubernetes side by side. Deploy services to either.
setup.k8sNote: Kubernetes will be installed after setup
setup.k8sNoteDesc: k3s (lightweight Kubernetes) can be installed from Admin Settings once setup is complete. Both orchestrators can coexist.
setup.createAdminDesc: This will be the super administrator of your platform.
setup.passwordMin8: Password must be at least 8 characters
setup.platformConfigDesc: Basic platform settings. You can change these later in admin settings.
setup.domainHint: Leave empty for localhost development. Can be changed later.
setup.jwtNote: A secure JWT secret will be auto-generated for signing authentication tokens.
setup.setupCompleteDesc: Your platform is ready. You're logged in as the super administrator.
setup.ensureDocker: Ensure Docker is installed and running on this node.
setup.runCommand: Run the following command on your server to install Docker:
setup.enableDocker: Then enable and start the Docker service:
setup.dockerRunning: Once Docker is running, proceed to the next step.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how {platformName} ("we", "us", or "our") collects, uses, and protects your information when you use our platform. We are committed to safeguarding your privacy and handling your data transparently.

infoCollect.title: 1. Information We Collect
infoCollect.body:
Account Information: When you create an account, we collect your name, email address, and password (stored as an Argon2 hash — we never store plaintext passwords).

Usage Data: We collect information about how you use the platform, including services deployed, resource usage (CPU, memory, storage), deployment logs, and API access patterns.

Payment Information: If you subscribe to a paid plan, payment details are processed and stored by Stripe. We do not store your full credit card number on our servers.

Technical Data: We automatically collect IP addresses, browser type, and device information when you access the dashboard. This data is used for security monitoring and rate limiting.

Service Data: Any data you store within your deployed containers, volumes, and backups is your data. We access it only to provide the service (e.g., running backups, restoring services) and never for other purposes.

howWeUse.title: 2. How We Use Your Information
howWeUse.body:
We use the information we collect to:

- Provide, maintain, and improve the {platformName} platform
- Process transactions and send billing-related communications
- Monitor platform security, detect abuse, and enforce rate limits
- Send service notifications (deployment status, health alerts, backup completion)
- Respond to support requests and communicate about your account
- Generate anonymized, aggregated analytics to improve our service

We do not sell your personal information to third parties. We do not use your data for advertising purposes.

dataStorage.title: 3. Data Storage & Security
dataStorage.body:
All sensitive data is encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM encryption. Backup archives are encrypted before storage. Database credentials and API keys stored in the platform are encrypted with a separate encryption key.

Your deployed services run in isolated Docker containers with dropped capabilities (CAP_DROP ALL), read-only filesystems where applicable, and network isolation via Docker Swarm overlay networks.

We use JWT tokens with HS256 algorithm pinning for authentication, with short-lived access tokens (15 minutes) and secure httpOnly cookies for refresh tokens.

All API communication is encrypted in transit via TLS. Internal node-to-node communication within the Swarm cluster uses encrypted overlay networks.

thirdParty.title: 4. Third-Party Services
thirdParty.body:
We use the following third-party services that may process your data:

- Stripe: Payment processing. Subject to Stripe's Privacy Policy.
- Email Provider (SMTP/Resend): Transactional emails (verification, password reset, deployment notifications). Only your email address and the message content are shared.
- Docker Hub / Container Registries: When deploying from public images. Only the image reference is sent.

We do not share your personal data with any other third parties unless required by law.

dataRetention.title: 5. Data Retention & Deletion
dataRetention.body:
Active accounts: Your data is retained as long as your account is active.

Deleted accounts: When you delete your account, we schedule deletion of all associated data including services, volumes, backups, and personal information. Soft-deleted records are permanently purged after 30 days.

Backups: Backup archives are retained according to your configured retention policy (default: 30 days, max 10 backups per schedule).

Audit logs: Security audit logs are retained for 90 days for security monitoring purposes.

You can request immediate deletion of all your data by contacting us.

yourRights.title: 6. Your Rights
yourRights.body:
You have the right to:

- Access: Request a copy of all personal data we hold about you.
- Correction: Update or correct your personal information via the dashboard.
- Deletion: Delete your account and all associated data.
- Export: Export your service configurations and data.
- Restriction: Request that we limit processing of your data.
- Portability: Receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format.

To exercise any of these rights, contact us at the address listed below or use the account settings in your dashboard.

cookies.title: 7. Cookies
cookies.body:
We use the following cookies:

- fleet_refresh: An httpOnly secure cookie containing your refresh token. Essential for authentication.
- fleet_locale: Stores your preferred language setting. Not sent to our servers.
- fleet_landing_theme: Stores your preferred theme (light/dark/system). Not sent to our servers.

We do not use tracking cookies, analytics cookies, or advertising cookies.

changes.title: 8. Changes to This Policy
changes.body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we make material changes, we will notify you via email or a prominent notice on the platform. Your continued use of {platformName} after changes are posted constitutes your acceptance of the updated policy.

contact.title: 9. Contact Us
contact.body:
If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or wish to exercise your data rights, please contact us at:

Email: {contactEmail}

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: By accessing or using {platformName} ("the Service"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service ("Terms"). If you do not agree to these Terms, do not use the Service.

description.title: 1. Description of Service
description.body:
{platformName} is an open-source, multi-tenant Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS) built on Docker Swarm. The Service provides container orchestration, automated deployments, domain management, backup management, billing integration, and related infrastructure management tools.

{platformName} may be self-hosted on your own infrastructure or accessed through a managed instance. These Terms apply to your use of the Service regardless of how it is deployed.

account.title: 2. Account Registration & Security
account.body:
You must provide accurate and complete information when creating an account. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials, including your password and API keys.

You must use a strong password containing at least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, and numeric characters. We strongly recommend enabling two-factor authentication (2FA).

You are responsible for all activity that occurs under your account. Notify us immediately if you suspect unauthorized access to your account.

We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these Terms or that we reasonably believe are being used for malicious purposes.

acceptableUse.title: 3. Acceptable Use Policy
acceptableUse.body:
You agree not to use the Service to:

- Deploy or host any content that is illegal, harmful, threatening, abusive, harassing, defamatory, or otherwise objectionable
- Run cryptocurrency mining, proof-of-work computation, or similar resource-intensive operations that are not legitimate application workloads
- Send spam, bulk unsolicited messages, or engage in phishing
- Distribute malware, viruses, or any other harmful software
- Attempt to gain unauthorized access to other accounts, services, or systems
- Interfere with or disrupt the Service or servers/networks connected to the Service
- Exceed reasonable resource usage in a manner that degrades service for other users
- Violate any applicable laws or regulations

We reserve the right to suspend or terminate services that violate this policy without prior notice.

availability.title: 4. Service Availability
availability.body:
We strive to maintain high availability of the Service but do not guarantee uninterrupted access. The Service may be temporarily unavailable due to:

- Scheduled maintenance (we will provide advance notice when possible)
- Infrastructure failures or network issues
- Security incidents requiring immediate response
- Force majeure events beyond our reasonable control

For self-hosted deployments, availability is dependent on your infrastructure and is your responsibility.

billing.title: 5. Billing & Payments
billing.body:
Free tier: {platformName} offers a free tier with limited resources. No payment information is required for the free tier.

Paid plans: Paid subscriptions are billed monthly through Stripe. By subscribing to a paid plan, you authorize us to charge your payment method on a recurring basis.

Upgrades and downgrades: Plan changes take effect at the start of the next billing cycle. Upgrading mid-cycle may result in prorated charges.

Failed payments: If a payment fails, we will attempt to retry. After continued payment failure, your account may be downgraded to the free tier and services exceeding free-tier limits may be suspended.

Refunds: We do not offer refunds for partial months of service. If you cancel, you retain access to paid features until the end of your current billing period.

ip.title: 6. Intellectual Property
ip.body:
{platformName} is open-source software licensed under its respective license. Your use of the {platformName} platform does not grant you any ownership rights to the platform's intellectual property.

You retain full ownership of all code, data, and content you deploy on the Service. We claim no intellectual property rights over your content.

dataPrivacy.title: 7. Data & Privacy
dataPrivacy.body:
Your use of the Service is also governed by our Privacy Policy, which describes how we collect, use, and protect your data. By using the Service, you consent to the data practices described in our Privacy Policy.

You are responsible for the data you store on the Service and for ensuring it complies with applicable data protection laws.

termination.title: 8. Termination
termination.body:
You may terminate your account at any time through the dashboard settings.

We may terminate or suspend your account if:

- You violate these Terms or the Acceptable Use Policy
- Your account is used for illegal or abusive purposes
- You fail to pay for a paid subscription after repeated attempts
- We are required to do so by law

Upon termination, your data will be scheduled for deletion according to our data retention policy. You may request a data export before termination.

liability.title: 9. Limitation of Liability
liability.body:
TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, {platformNameUpper} AND ITS OPERATORS SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO LOSS OF PROFITS, DATA, OR BUSINESS OPPORTUNITIES.

OUR TOTAL LIABILITY FOR ANY CLAIMS ARISING FROM OR RELATED TO THE SERVICE SHALL NOT EXCEED THE AMOUNT YOU PAID US IN THE 12 MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM.

THE SERVICE IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND "AS AVAILABLE" WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, WHETHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED.

governingLaw.title: 10. Governing Law
governingLaw.body: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with applicable laws. Any disputes arising from these Terms or your use of the Service shall be resolved through good-faith negotiation first, and if necessary, through binding arbitration or in the courts of competent jurisdiction.

changes.title: 11. Changes to These Terms
changes.body:
We may update these Terms from time to time. When we make material changes, we will notify you via email or a prominent notice on the platform at least 30 days before the changes take effect.

Your continued use of the Service after the updated Terms take effect constitutes your acceptance of the changes. If you do not agree to the updated Terms, you must stop using the Service.

contact.title: 12. Contact Us
contact.body:
If you have questions about these Terms of Service, please contact us at:

Email: {contactEmail}

docs.title: Docs
docs.gettingStartedIntro: Fleet is an open-source, multi-tenant PaaS built on Docker Swarm. This guide will help you set up your first Fleet cluster and deploy a service.
docs.prereq1: A Linux server (Ubuntu 22.04+, Debian 12+, Rocky 9+, or Fedora 39+ recommended)
docs.prereq2: Root or sudo access (the install script handles Docker, NFS, and all dependencies automatically)
docs.prereq3: A domain name pointed to your server (optional but recommended)
docs.prereqNote: Docker, PostgreSQL, and all required dependencies are installed automatically by the Fleet install script. Pre-installed versions will be detected and reused.
docs.installScript: Install Fleet using the automated install script:
docs.initCluster: Initialize your Fleet cluster on the leader node:
docs.loginCli: Log in to the Fleet CLI:
docs.deployDone: That's it! Your service is now running on your Fleet cluster. You can view it in the dashboard or manage it with the CLI.
docs.cliReferenceIntro: Complete reference for all Fleet CLI commands.
docs.configIntro: Fleet is configured through environment variables. Below are the key settings.
docs.configDatabaseUrl: Connection string for PostgreSQL or MySQL
docs.configJwtSecret: Secret key for signing JWT tokens
docs.configValkeyUrl: Valkey/Redis connection URL for job queues
docs.configNfsServer: NFS server address for shared storage
docs.integrationsIntro: Add a "Deploy on Fleet" button to any GitHub repository. Users click the button, land on a pre-filled deploy form, and launch the service in a few clicks.
docs.deployButtonDesc: The deploy button is a badge that links to your Fleet instance's one-click deploy page. It works like Heroku, Vercel, or Railway deploy buttons.
docs.addToReadme: Add this to your repository's README:
docs.replaceUrls: Replace {landingSite} with your Fleet landing page URL and {dashboard} with your Fleet dashboard URL.
docs.urlParamsDesc: The deploy URL supports the following query parameters:
docs.paramBranchDesc: Branch to deploy. Falls back to fleet.json default, then the repo's default branch
docs.paramEnvDesc: Pre-fill environment variables. Use multiple {format} params for multiple variables
docs.howStep1: User clicks the deploy button in your README
docs.howStep2: Fleet fetches {file} from your repo (if it exists) and pre-fills the deploy form
docs.howStep3: User reviews the configuration, fills in any required env vars, and clicks Deploy
docs.howStep4: Fleet creates the service, clones the repo, builds and deploys it
docs.howNote: If the user is not logged in, they'll be redirected to the login page first, then returned to the deploy form after authentication.
docs.withBranchAndEnv: With branch and pre-filled env vars:
docs.fleetJsonDesc: The {file} file is an optional manifest placed in the root of your repository. When present, it enriches the deploy experience with metadata, environment variable descriptions, port configuration, and build settings.
docs.fieldNameDesc: Display name for the app (max 100 chars)
docs.fieldDescriptionDesc: Short description shown on the deploy page (max 500 chars)
docs.fieldIconDesc: URL to an app icon displayed on the deploy page
docs.fieldBuildFileDesc: Path to the Dockerfile or docker-compose.yml (max 200 chars)
docs.fieldBranchDesc: Default branch to deploy if not specified in the URL
docs.envVarsDesc: Each key in the {env} object maps to an environment variable. The value can be a plain string (used as the default value) or an object with the following properties:
docs.propDescriptionDesc: Human-readable help text shown next to the input
docs.propRequiredDesc: Whether the variable must be filled (default: true)
docs.propGenerateDesc: Auto-generate a random 32-byte hex string. Useful for secrets, tokens, and encryption keys
docs.portMappingsDesc: Each entry in the {ports} array defines a port mapping:
docs.bestPractice1: Use {code} for secrets — never commit default secret values
docs.bestPractice2: Add {code} to every env var — it helps users understand what each variable does
docs.bestPractice3: Set {code} for optional env vars — all env vars are required by default
docs.bestPractice4: Always include a {code} in your repo so Fleet can build and run it
docs.bestPracticeNo1: Never put actual secret values in {code} — it's committed to your repo and publicly visible

infrastructure.title: Infrastructure
infrastructure.desc: Initialize and manage your Fleet cluster.
infrastructure.initDesc: Initialize the first node. Sets up Docker Swarm, creates overlay networks, and generates secrets.
infrastructure.joinDesc: Join this node to an existing Fleet swarm cluster.
infrastructure.statusDesc: Show the current cluster status, including swarm nodes and running services.

storage.title: Distributed Storage
storage.intro: Fleet includes a production-grade distributed storage system that replicates data across multiple nodes with automatic failover and self-healing. This guide covers provider selection, node setup, hardware recommendations, and data migration.
storage.overviewDesc: Fleet's storage system handles two categories of data through a unified provider abstraction:
storage.volumeStorageDesc: Block or filesystem storage that mounts directly into Docker containers. Used for persistent application data, databases, and user files. Providers:
storage.objectStorageDesc: Key-value blob storage for uploads, backups, and build artifacts. Providers:
storage.defaultStoragePart2: (single-node NFS for volumes, filesystem for objects). This is ideal for development and single-server deployments. For production, switch to a distributed provider to get replication, failover, and self-healing.
storage.providerAbstractionNote: All existing services continue working when you switch providers. Fleet's storage manager abstracts the underlying provider, so volume mounts, backups, and uploads work identically regardless of the backend.
storage.localNfsDesc: Single-node storage using the local filesystem and NFS exports. No replication. Best for development, testing, and single-server deployments.
storage.glusterDesc: Distributed replicated filesystem. Data is mirrored across 2-3 nodes automatically. POSIX-compatible, auto-heals when a node recovers, and integrates with Docker via the GlusterFS volume plugin. Ideal for bare metal servers and cloud VMs.
storage.glusterFeature3: Same stack on cloud VMs and bare metal (no vendor lock-in)
storage.cephDesc: Enterprise-grade block storage built on Ceph RADOS. Scales to exabytes with erasure coding, thin provisioning, and snapshots. Best for large-scale datacenter deployments with dedicated storage infrastructure.
storage.minioDesc: S3-compatible object storage that runs on your own nodes. Supports distributed mode with erasure coding across multiple disks/nodes. No vendor lock-in, full data sovereignty.
storage.awsS3Desc: Amazon S3 or any S3-compatible service (Backblaze B2, Wasabi, DigitalOcean Spaces, Cloudflare R2). Uses the official AWS SDK. Supports custom endpoints for S3-compatible providers.
storage.gcsDesc: Native Google Cloud Storage integration. Supports workload identity (no key file needed on GCP VMs) and service account authentication. Multi-region and single-region storage classes.
storage.nodeSetupDesc: Each node in your storage cluster needs the appropriate provider software installed. Fleet provides an automated setup script that handles installation, firewall rules, and kernel tuning.
storage.quickInstallDesc: Run on each node that will participate in the storage cluster:
storage.interactiveInstallerNote: The interactive installer will prompt you to choose a provider. Or specify options directly:
storage.glusterSetupDesc: The script installs GlusterFS, creates the brick directory, starts the daemon, configures firewall rules (ports 24007-24008, 49152-49251), and installs the Docker GlusterFS volume plugin.
storage.cephNotepart1: This script installs the Ceph client tools (rbd, ceph CLI). A full Ceph cluster requires separate deployment using
storage.cephNotePart2: , or Rook (for Kubernetes). Fleet connects to an existing Ceph cluster via the monitor addresses and keyring.
storage.providerFlagDesc: Provider to install: glusterfs, ceph, minio, or all
storage.afterInstallDesc: Once storage software is installed on all nodes:
storage.afterInstallStep1Part1: Go to your Fleet admin panel →
storage.afterInstallStep3: Add each storage node's hostname and IP address
storage.afterInstallStep4: Run the connectivity test to verify all nodes are reachable
storage.afterInstallStep5Part2: to form the cluster and create the trusted storage pool
storage.afterInstallStep6: Optionally migrate existing data from local storage
storage.hardwareDesc: Storage node performance depends heavily on disk I/O and network throughput. Below are recommendations for different deployment scales.
storage.minimumRequirementsDesc: The absolute minimum for a 3-node distributed storage cluster:
storage.smallScaleItem4: GlusterFS with replica-3 + MinIO on same nodes
storage.smallScaleItem5: Shared storage + compute nodes are fine at this scale
storage.mediumScaleItem3: 10 Gbps network (bonded or dedicated storage VLAN)
storage.mediumScaleItem4: GlusterFS or Ceph + MinIO or S3
storage.mediumScaleItem5: Dedicated storage nodes recommended for I/O performance
storage.largeScaleItem1: 10+ dedicated storage nodes, 16+ cores / 32+ GB RAM each
storage.largeScaleItem2: 4-8 TB NVMe SSD or HDD (with SSD journal) per node
storage.largeScaleItem3: 25 Gbps or higher, dedicated storage network
storage.largeScaleItem4: Ceph RBD strongly recommended (erasure coding reduces storage overhead)
storage.largeScaleItem5: Cloud object storage (S3/GCS) for backups and cold data
storage.largeScaleItem6: Separate monitor and OSD nodes for Ceph
storage.petabyteScaleItem1: Ceph is the only volume provider that scales to this level
storage.petabyteScaleItem2: 50+ OSD nodes with high-density disk shelves (JBOD)
storage.petabyteScaleItem5: Erasure coding profiles (e.g., 4+2) to reduce replication overhead from 3x to 1.5x
storage.petabyteScaleItem6: Use cloud object storage (S3/GCS) for archival and cross-region replication
storage.nvmeSsdTip: Best overall performance. Use for volumes that serve databases or high-IOPS workloads.
storage.sataSsdTip: Good for general-purpose storage. Sufficient for most application volumes and object storage.
storage.hddSsdTip: Cost-effective for large capacity. Use for backups, cold storage, and archival. Add a small SSD for the Ceph OSD journal/WAL to improve write performance.
storage.avoidMixingDiskTypesDesc: within a Ceph pool — performance bottlenecks occur when fast and slow disks share a pool.
storage.networkTip1: Storage replication is network-intensive. A dedicated storage VLAN or separate NIC is strongly recommended for clusters with 5+ nodes.
storage.networkTip2: Keep storage nodes in the same datacenter or availability zone. Cross-region replication should use object storage (S3/GCS), not volume replication.
storage.networkTip3: Latency between storage nodes should be under 1ms. Storage performance degrades rapidly above 5ms round-trip latency.
storage.migrationDesc: Fleet supports zero-downtime migration between any storage providers. You can migrate from local to distributed, between distributed providers, or from cloud to self-hosted (and vice versa).
storage.migrationInventoryDesc: Fleet scans all existing volumes, uploads, and backups to calculate the total migration scope.
storage.migrationCopyDesc: Data is streamed from the source provider to the destination provider. Volumes are copied via rsync/direct copy, objects are streamed bucket-by-bucket.
storage.migrationVerifyDesc: Checksums are compared between source and destination to ensure data integrity.
storage.migrationSwitchoverDesc: The active provider is atomically switched to the new destination. Services experience a brief pause (typically under 5 seconds) during switchover.
storage.migrationCleanupDesc: After a configurable grace period, data on the old provider can be removed.
storage.migrationWizardDesc: The easiest way to migrate is through the admin panel:
storage.wizardStep2: Select your target volume and object providers
storage.wizardStep3Part1: Review the migration summary and click
storage.wizardStep4Part1: Monitor progress in real-time — you can
storage.wizardStep5Part1: After completion, verify everything is working. If needed, click
storage.wizardStep5Part2: to revert to the previous provider.
storage.migrationTip: For large datasets (10+ TB), consider running the migration during off-peak hours. While services remain online during migration, the I/O overhead of copying data can impact performance on the source provider.

networking.title: Networking & Ports
networking.intro: Fleet uses several network ports for communication between nodes, serving traffic, and providing file access. This guide covers which ports to open, firewall configuration, and SFTP access setup.
networking.requiredPortsDesc: The following ports must be accessible for Fleet to operate correctly. Ports are categorized by their purpose.
networking.publicPortsDesc: These ports must be open to the internet (or to your users' networks):
networking.httpsDesc: Secure web traffic, dashboard, and API
networking.sftpDesc: Secure file transfer for upload-based services
networking.swarmPortsDesc: These ports are used for node-to-node communication within the Docker Swarm cluster. They should only be open between your nodes, not to the public internet:
networking.swarmManagementDesc: Docker Swarm cluster management and Raft consensus
networking.nodeDiscoveryDesc: Container network discovery and gossip protocol
networking.overlayNetworkDesc: VXLAN overlay network for cross-node container communication
networking.nfsDesc: Network File System for shared volume storage
networking.optionalStoragePortsDesc: Only needed if you use distributed storage providers:
networking.firewallDesc: The Fleet install script automatically configures firewall rules for the required ports. If you need to set them up manually, use the commands below.
networking.securityTipPart1: For production deployments, restrict Swarm ports (2377, 7946, 4789) and NFS (2049) to only your cluster node IPs rather than opening them to all traffic. Use
networking.sftpAccessDesc: Fleet includes a built-in SFTP server that provides secure file access to services using custom upload (file-based deployment). Users can connect via any SFTP client to manage their uploaded files directly.
networking.sftpHowItem1Part1: SFTP server runs embedded in the Fleet API process on port
networking.sftpHowItem2Part2: the same keys used for the REST API and CLI
networking.sftpHowItem3Part2: to the specific service's upload directory — no cross-account or cross-service access
networking.sftpHowItem4: Built-in rate limiting on failed auth attempts and per-account connection limits protect against abuse
networking.connectingDescPart1: Use any SFTP client (FileZilla, WinSCP, Cyberduck, or the command line). The username format is
networking.connectingDescPart2: and the password is your API key.
networking.perAccountIsolationDesc: Each SFTP session is restricted to its service's upload directory. Path traversal is blocked.
networking.bruteForceDesc: After 10 failed login attempts from an IP within 60 seconds, further attempts are blocked for that IP.
networking.connectionLimitsDesc: Global limit of 10,000 connections and 100 per account (both configurable) prevent resource exhaustion.
networking.sshEncryptionDesc: All traffic is encrypted via SSH (ed25519 host key, auto-generated on first startup).
networking.apiKeyAuthDesc: Passwords are verified against argon2-hashed API keys in the database. Expired keys are rejected.
networking.disablingSftpDesc: If you don't need SFTP access, disable it by setting the environment variable:
